revision.text:
[[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = D1:D36 M44-X1 N14-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Tepi-a Sopdet
''Tpj-ˁ Spd.t''
''Forerunner of Sopdet'' |BILD1 = Position Beta Cma.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation “Sopdet” ([[:en:Canis Major|Canis Major]]) }} '''Tepi-a Sopdet''' is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan star). == Background == This decan corresponds to [[:en:Beta Canis Majoris|Murzim]], the forerunner of [[:en:Sirius|Sirius]]. The traditional [[:en:Arabic|Arabic language]] name, "Harbinger," likely refers to its position, as it rises before Sirius and heralds the brightest star in the night sky. However, this role does not apply to [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] because Murzim is significantly dimmer than Sirius and requires a {{ill|Altitude|de|Sehungsbogen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Altitude|qq=|WD=Q1521163}} of at least 13° to become heliacally visible, or approximately 55 minutes before the sun rises. Furthermore, Murzim requires a minimum horizon altitude of 1.7° due to atmospheric refraction. The same conditions apply to {{ill|Acronychal setting|de|Akronychisch|1=Acronychic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychal setting|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}}. The entries in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] regarding decan positions can therefore only refer to the acronychal [[:en:Culmination|Culmination (astronomy)]], as only in this context could Murzim Rank before Sirius in the decan lists. During the era of the {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Murzim reached a culmination altitude of approximately 36.6° in Ancient Egypt. Sirius itself was part of the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Sopdet (constellation)|de|Sopdet (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sopdet (constellation)|qq=|WD=Q1418302}}. {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}} notes the decan star "Tepi-a Sopdet" mentioned in the [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]], which in the seventh year of [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] was listed as the 34th decan with the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] assigned to the {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=6th Peret IV|de|6. Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} (approximately June 15/21); the [[:en:Culmination|Culmination (astronomy)]] for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the acronychal setting for the 26th Peret I. The recorded period of invisibility lasted the canonical 70 days.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne''. p. 66. == Dating of Tepi-a Sopdet (Book of Nut System) == {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="7" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Dating of Tepi-a Sopdet in Memphis''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !Year ![[Culmination (astronomy)|Acronychal
Culmination]] ![[Acronychal|Acronychal
Setting]] ![[Heliacal rising|Heliacal
Rising]]The altitude values refer to the end of the respective night hour. Heliacal rising occurs before sunrise, acronychal setting, and acronychal culmination after sunset. !Remarks |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1873 BCE | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
January 11–12
Altitude 30.3°Actual culmination: 36.5°, approximately 2.75 hours after sunset. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=26th Peret I|de|26. Peret I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
April 11–12
Altitude 10.4°The observation of the setting was only possible towards the end of the decade. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=6th Peret IV|de|6. Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 20–21
Altitude 2.3°On the 10th decade day, the altitude was 9.3°. A heliacal observation during the decade was therefore not possible. | style="text-align:center" | [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | style="text-align:center" | 1541 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret I
January 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret IV
April 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=29th Shemu II|de|29. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 23–24 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1463 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 8th Peret II
January 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 8th Shemu I
April 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 18th Shemu III
June 24–25 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=|WD=Q142353}} |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62 and 398. == References == {{translated page|de|Tepi-a Sopdet|version=210539685|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{wikidata link|Q1653714}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 20–21
Altitude 2.3°On the 10th decade day, the altitude was 9.3°. A heliacal observation during the decade was therefore not possible. | style="text-align:center" | [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | style="text-align:center" | 1541 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret I
January 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret IV
April 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=29th Shemu II|de|29. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 23–24 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1463 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 8th Peret II
January 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 8th Shemu I
April 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 18th Shemu III
June 24–25 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=|WD=Q142353}} |- |} == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = D1:D36 M44-X1 N14-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Tepi-a Sopdet
''Tpj-ˁ Spd.t''
''Forerunner of Sopdet'' |BILD1 = Position Beta Cma.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation “Sopdet” ({{ill|Canis Major|en|Canis Major|WD=Q10538|lt=Canis Major}}) }} '''Tepi-a Sopdet''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan star). == Background == This decan corresponds to {{ill|Beta Canis Majoris|en|Beta Canis Majoris|WD=Q13415|lt=Murzim}}, the forerunner of {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|WD=Q3409|lt=Sirius}}. The traditional {{ill|Arabic|en|Arabic|WD=Q13955|lt=Arabic language}} name, "Harbinger," likely refers to its position, as it rises before Sirius and heralds the brightest star in the night sky. However, this role does not apply to {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} because Murzim is significantly dimmer than Sirius and requires a {{ill|Altitude|de|Sehungsbogen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Altitude|qq=|WD=Q1521163}} of at least 13° to become heliacally visible, or approximately 55 minutes before the sun rises. Furthermore, Murzim requires a minimum horizon altitude of 1.7° due to atmospheric refraction. The same conditions apply to {{ill|Acronychal setting|de|Akronychisch|1=Acronychic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychal setting|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}}. The entries in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}} regarding decan positions can therefore only refer to the acronychal {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=Culmination (astronomy)}}, as only in this context could Murzim Rank before Sirius in the decan lists. During the era of the {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Murzim reached a culmination altitude of approximately 36.6° in Ancient Egypt. Sirius itself was part of the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Sopdet (constellation)|de|Sopdet (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sopdet (constellation)|qq=|WD=Q1418302}}. {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}} notes the decan star "Tepi-a Sopdet" mentioned in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}}, which in the seventh year of {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} was listed as the 34th decan with the {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} assigned to the {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=6th Peret IV|de|6. Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} (approximately June 15/21); the {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=Culmination (astronomy)}} for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the acronychal setting for the 26th Peret I. The recorded period of invisibility lasted the canonical 70 days.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne''. p. 66. == Dating of Tepi-a Sopdet (Book of Nut System) == {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="7" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Dating of Tepi-a Sopdet in Memphis''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !Year ![[Culmination (astronomy)|Acronychal
Culmination]] ![[Acronychal|Acronychal
Setting]] ![[Heliacal rising|Heliacal
Rising]]The altitude values refer to the end of the respective night hour. Heliacal rising occurs before sunrise, acronychal setting, and acronychal culmination after sunset. !Remarks |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1873 BCE | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
January 11–12
Altitude 30.3°Actual culmination: 36.5°, approximately 2.75 hours after sunset. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=26th Peret I|de|26. Peret I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
April 11–12
Altitude 10.4°The observation of the setting was only possible towards the end of the decade. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=6th Peret IV|de|6. Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 20–21
Altitude 2.3°On the 10th decade day, the altitude was 9.3°. A heliacal observation during the decade was therefore not possible. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | style="text-align:center" | 1541 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret I
January 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret IV
April 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=29th Shemu II|de|29. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 23–24 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1463 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 8th Peret II
January 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 8th Shemu I
April 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 18th Shemu III
June 24–25 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=|WD=Q142353}} |- |} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62 and 398. == References == {{translated page|de|Tepi-a Sopdet|version=210539685|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{wikidata link|Q1653714}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

A heliacal observation during the decade was therefore not possible. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | style="text-align:center" | 1541 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret I

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62 and 398. == References == {{translated page|de|Tepi-a Sopdet|version=210539685|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{wikidata link|Q1653714}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text:
[[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = D1:D36 M44-X1 N14-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Tepi-a Sopdet
''Tpj-ˁ Spd.t''
''Forerunner of Sopdet'' |BILD1 = Position Beta Cma.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation “Sopdet” ({{ill|Canis Major|en|Canis Major|WD=Q10538|lt=Canis Major}}) }} '''Tepi-a Sopdet''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan star). == Background == This decan corresponds to {{ill|Beta Canis Majoris|en|Beta Canis Majoris|WD=Q13415|lt=Murzim}}, the forerunner of {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|WD=Q3409|lt=Sirius}}. The traditional {{ill|Arabic|en|Arabic|WD=Q13955|lt=Arabic language}} name, "Harbinger," likely refers to its position, as it rises before Sirius and heralds the brightest star in the night sky. However, this role does not apply to {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} because Murzim is significantly dimmer than Sirius and requires a {{ill|Altitude|de|Sehungsbogen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Altitude|qq=|WD=Q1521163}} of at least 13° to become heliacally visible, or approximately 55 minutes before the sun rises. Furthermore, Murzim requires a minimum horizon altitude of 1.7° due to atmospheric refraction. The same conditions apply to {{ill|Acronychal setting|de|Akronychisch|1=Acronychic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychal setting|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}}. The entries in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}} regarding decan positions can therefore only refer to the acronychal {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=Culmination (astronomy)}}, as only in this context could Murzim Rank before Sirius in the decan lists. During the era of the {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Murzim reached a culmination altitude of approximately 36.6° in Ancient Egypt. Sirius itself was part of the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Sopdet (constellation)|de|Sopdet (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sopdet (constellation)|qq=|WD=Q1418302}}. {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}} notes the decan star "Tepi-a Sopdet" mentioned in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut}}, which in the seventh year of {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} was listed as the 34th decan with the {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} assigned to the {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=6th Peret IV|de|6. Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} (approximately June 15/21); the {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=Culmination (astronomy)}} for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the acronychal setting for the 26th Peret I. The recorded period of invisibility lasted the canonical 70 days.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne''. p. 66. == Dating of Tepi-a Sopdet (Book of Nut System) == {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="7" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Dating of Tepi-a Sopdet in Memphis''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !Year ![[Culmination (astronomy)|Acronychal
Culmination]] ![[Acronychal|Acronychal
Setting]] ![[Heliacal rising|Heliacal
Rising]]The altitude values refer to the end of the respective night hour. Heliacal rising occurs before sunrise, acronychal setting, and acronychal culmination after sunset. !Remarks |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1873 BCE | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
January 11–12
Altitude 30.3°Actual culmination: 36.5°, approximately 2.75 hours after sunset. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=26th Peret I|de|26. Peret I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
April 11–12
Altitude 10.4°The observation of the setting was only possible towards the end of the decade. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=6th Peret IV|de|6. Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 20–21
Altitude 2.3°On the 10th decade day, the altitude was 9.3°. A heliacal observation during the decade was therefore not possible. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | style="text-align:center" | 1541 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret I
January 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret IV
April 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=29th Shemu II|de|29. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 23–24 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1463 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 8th Peret II
January 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 8th Shemu I
April 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 18th Shemu III
June 24–25 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=|WD=Q142353}} |- |} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62 and 398. == References == {{translated page|de|Tepi-a Sopdet|version=210539685|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{wikidata link|Q1653714}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages]]

Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 20–21
Altitude 2.3°On the 10th decade day, the altitude was 9.3°. A heliacal observation during the decade was therefore not possible. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | style="text-align:center" | 1541 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret I

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. revision.text:
[[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = D1:D36 M44-X1 N14-N33 |NAME-TRANSKRIPTION = Tepi-a Sopdet
''Tpj-ˁ Spd.t''
''Forerunner of Sopdet'' |BILD1 = Position Beta Cma.png |BILD1-BREITE = 250px |BILD1-BESCHREIBUNG = Constellation “Sopdet” ({{ill|Canis Major|en|Canis Major|WD=Q10538|lt=Canis Major|1=Canis Major}}) }} '''Tepi-a Sopdet''' is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt|1=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan|1=Decan}}, which comprised only one {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan star). == Background == This decan corresponds to {{ill|Beta Canis Majoris|en|Beta Canis Majoris|WD=Q13415|lt=Murzim|1=Beta Canis Majoris}}, the forerunner of {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|WD=Q3409|lt=Sirius|1=Sirius}}. The traditional {{ill|Arabic|en|Arabic|WD=Q13955|lt=Arabic language|1=Arabic}} name, "Harbinger," likely refers to its position, as it rises before Sirius and heralds the brightest star in the night sky. However, this role does not apply to {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} because Murzim is significantly dimmer than Sirius and requires a {{ill|Altitude|de|Sehungsbogen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Altitude|qq=|WD=Q1521163|1=Q1521163}} of at least 13° to become heliacally visible, or approximately 55 minutes before the sun rises. Furthermore, Murzim requires a minimum horizon altitude of 1.7° due to atmospheric refraction. The same conditions apply to {{ill|Acronychal setting|de|Akronychisch|1=Acronychic|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychal setting|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}}. The entries in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut|1=Book of Nut}} regarding decan positions can therefore only refer to the acronychal {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=Culmination (astronomy)|1=Culmination}}, as only in this context could Murzim Rank before Sirius in the decan lists. During the era of the {{ill|Diagonal star clock|de|Diagonalsternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Diagonal star clock|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, Murzim reached a culmination altitude of approximately 36.6° in Ancient Egypt. Sirius itself was part of the Ancient Egyptian constellation {{ill|Sopdet (constellation)|de|Sopdet (Sternbild)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sopdet (constellation)|qq=|WD=Q1418302|1=Sopdet (constellation)}}. {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}} notes the decan star "Tepi-a Sopdet" mentioned in the {{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|WD=Q2005559|lt=Book of Nut|1=Book of Nut}}, which in the seventh year of {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} was listed as the 34th decan with the {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} assigned to the {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=6th Peret IV|de|6. Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} (approximately June 15/21); the {{ill|Culmination|en|Culmination|WD=Q611114|lt=Culmination (astronomy)|1=Culmination}} for the {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the acronychal setting for the 26th Peret I. The recorded period of invisibility lasted the canonical 70 days.Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne''. p. 66. == Dating of Tepi-a Sopdet (Book of Nut System) == {| class="wikitable" style="width: 98%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} |colspan="7" align=center style="background:#FFE39B"|'''Dating of Tepi-a Sopdet in Memphis''' |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} !Year ![[Culmination (astronomy)|Acronychal
Culmination]] ![[Acronychal|Acronychal
Setting]] ![[Heliacal rising|Heliacal
Rising]]The altitude values refer to the end of the respective night hour. Heliacal rising occurs before sunrise, acronychal setting, and acronychal culmination after sunset. !Remarks |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1873 BCE | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Achet#Dating|lt=26th Achet II|de|26. Achet II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achet#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
January 11–12
Altitude 30.3°Actual culmination: 36.5°, approximately 2.75 hours after sunset. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=26th Peret I|de|26. Peret I|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
April 11–12
Altitude 10.4°The observation of the setting was only possible towards the end of the decade. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Peret#Dating|lt=6th Peret IV|de|6. Peret IV|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Peret#Dating|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 20–21
Altitude 2.3°On the 10th decade day, the altitude was 9.3°. A heliacal observation during the decade was therefore not possible. | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} |- bgcolor="#f5f5f5" | style="text-align:center" | 1541 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret I
January 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | 19th Peret IV
April 14–15 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=Shemu#Dating|lt=29th Shemu II|de|29. Schemu II|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
June 23–24 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717|1=Ebers calendar}} |- bgcolor="#fffaf0" | style="text-align:center" | 1463 BCE | style="text-align:center" | 8th Peret II
January 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 8th Shemu I
April 15–16 | style="text-align:center" | 18th Shemu III
June 24–25 | style="text-align:center" | {{ill|Star clock|lt=Star clock|lt=Ramesside star clocks|de|Ramessidische Sternuhren|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ramesside star clocks|qq=|WD=Q142353|1=Star clock}} |- |} == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, p. 95. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62 and 398. == References == {{translated page|de|Tepi-a Sopdet|version=210539685|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Tepi-a Sopdet]] {{wikidata link|Q1653714}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
